channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rotary Club of Jinja launches 4th Run for the Nile to raise sh550 million for environmental conservation
item.description: The Rotary Club of Jinja has officially launched the fourth edition of the Run for the Nile, an annual event aimed at mobilising resources and raising awareness to address environmental degradation in...

item.title: England vs Pakistan T20 World Cup Match: Live Score Update
item.description: The Telegraph's coverage of the ICC T20 World Cup match between England and Pakistan is currently inaccessible due to a server access restriction. Readers attempting to view live scores or match updat...

item.title: UK Front Pages Highlight Political Controversies Amid Reporting Restrictions
item.description: This BBC News article provides an overview of how several major UK newspapers are leading their front pages, while also noting the strict reporting restrictions in place due to polling day in the Gort...

item.title: US citizen Tommy Schaefer deported from Indonesia after serving 11 years for 2014 Bali murder
item.description: Indonesia has deported American citizen Tommy Schaefer, now 33, from Bali after he served 11 years in prison for his role in the 2014 murder of Sheila von Wiese-Mack, the mother of his then-girlfriend...

item.title: Study finds vegetarian diet linked to lower risk of several cancers but higher risk of others
item.description: A comprehensive study by researchers at the University of Oxford has found that following a vegetarian diet can lower the risk of certain cancers, including pancreatic, breast, prostate, kidney, and m...

item.title: UK temperatures drop after record February warmth, but spring blooms expected
item.description: The UK recently experienced its warmest February day since 2019, with temperatures at Kew Gardens in London reaching 18.6°C and parts of Kent and Suffolk topping 18°C. These unusually high temperature...

item.title: Heart-Healthy Morning Drinks That Can Support Cholesterol Levels
item.description: For individuals with high LDL cholesterol, incorporating certain morning beverages into your routine can help support heart health. Dietitians recommend pomegranate juice as a top choice due to its hi...

item.title: Tensions rise as Dubois and Wardley clash at press conference ahead of WBO title fight
item.description: Daniel Dubois and Fabio Wardley came face-to-face at a heated press conference in London ahead of their WBO heavyweight title bout scheduled for 9 May at Manchester's Co-op Live Arena. The event, held...

item.title: Green Party's historic victory in Gorton and Denton by-election signals growing challenge to Labour
item.description: The Green Party's victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election has sent shockwaves through British politics, with Zack Polanski and his team celebrating a stunning result. The Greens overturned a 13,0...

item.title: Australian Prime Minister Apologizes for Describing Abuse Survivor Grace Tame as 'Difficult'
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has issued an apology after describing Grace Tame, a survivor of child sexual abuse and 2021 Australian of the Year, as 'difficult.' The comment was made dur...

item.title: Taiwanese Designer Turns Tears into Ammunition in Conceptual Art Piece
item.description: At Dutch Design Week, Taiwanese designer Yi-Fei Chen presented an innovative concept called Tear Gun. This unique project takes something often seen as a symbol of vulnerability—tears—and transforms t...

item.title: Aston Martin to Cut 600 Jobs After Losses Rise to Nearly £500m
item.description: Aston Martin has announced plans to cut approximately 20% of its global workforce after reporting a sharp increase in annual losses. The luxury car manufacturer revealed that its net losses widened by...

item.title: Winston Churchill statue in Westminster defaced with political graffiti
item.description: In Westminster, pro-Palestine activists have vandalised the statue of Sir Winston Churchill, which has stood outside the Houses of Parliament since 1973. Overnight, red paint was used to scrawl slogan...

item.title: Labour criticised for response to heavy defeat in Gorton and Denton byelection
item.description: Marina Hyde delivers a scathing critique of Labour’s reaction to its poor performance in the Gorton and Denton byelection, where the party fell to third place despite the seat being regarded as a hist...

item.title: Pressure Mounts on Starmer as Labour Faces Backlash Over By-Election Loss
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is facing growing criticism from within the Labour Party following a poor by-election result in Gorton and Denton. High-profile figures, including former deputy prime minister Angela ...

item.title: EU Project Develops PFAS-Free and Cost-Effective Green Hydrogen Technology
item.description: Green hydrogen is considered a crucial component for reducing reliance on fossil fuels, but current production methods face major challenges. Proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolysis, one of the le...

item.title: Gorton and Denton by-election sees tight contest amid concerns over family voting
item.description: The Gorton and Denton by-election, a key test of Sir Keir Starmer’s leadership, has entered a tense counting phase following the close of polls. Although Labour secured over 50 per cent in the 2024 ge...

item.title: Ultrahuman Launches Ring Pro With 15-Day Battery Life as It Seeks U.S. Market Reentry After Patent Dispute
item.description: Ultrahuman has introduced the Ring Pro, its third-generation smart ring, as it works to restore its position in the U.S. market following a patent dispute with rival Oura. The Bengaluru-based wearable...

item.title: UK asylum applications fall slightly in 2025 despite rise in Channel crossings
item.description: In 2025, the number of asylum claims in the United Kingdom decreased by 4%, totalling 100,625 applications, according to Home Office data. Despite this overall decline, arrivals via small boats crossi...

item.title: Ian Huntley in Serious Condition Following Attack at HMP Frankland
item.description: Ian Huntley, the convicted murderer of 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in Soham in 2002, remains in hospital in a serious condition following an alleged attack by another inmate at HMP Fr...
